body_txt:
When someone's inner darkness doesn't quite take over someone , but it does "escape" their body and rampage. Fighting someone else's Enemy Without is tricky, as often it will either kill the person projecting it if it dies , or it will just resurrect itself until the actual darkness in the hero's heart is dealt with by the hero themselves. Often symbolically represents repression, and the hero's refusal to acknowledge the darkness within or some other aspect of themselves. Victory is achieved half the time via " reintegrating " with it. The other half of the time it can be seen as representing some inner demon, and thus, it must be abandoned, purged, or confronted and conquered. Sometimes this battle will occur inside someone's own head in a dreamscape , making it both the Enemy Within and the Enemy Without at the same time. It may require saying " I'm Not Afraid of You !" to weaken it enough to beat. This trope is very similar to The Heartless , Made of Evil , and Literal Split Personality . It is distinguished from The Heartless by having some personal connection to the person/people it was created from, from Made of Evil because the Enemy may be flesh and bone, and from Literal Split Personality by leaving behind a clearly-defined original character. This trope can also apply to an Ineffectual Sympathetic Villain who struggles between the line of good and evil, with the split allowing him to simultaneously perform a Heel–Face Turn with his good half while his evil side proves he's a Not-So-Harmless Villain . See also: Evil Twin , Enemy Within , Ghost in the Machine , Self-Constructed Being , Shadow Archetype , Indulgent Fantasy Segue , Mental Monster , Tulpa , Powering Villain Realization , and Residual Evil Entity . Very dangerous in combination with Superpowered Evil Side .
The "evil" is usually part of a character's Soul Anatomy . And please don't make that lame "Without what?" joke. We've heard it before .
